_comment: This policy is evaluated by the core PolicyEngine (adapter-agnostic layer).
Because secret commands parse as type 'UNKNOWN', matching is done on that type.
Fine-grained secret-level rules (leak detection, exfiltration, wildcard delete,
raw output on production) are enforced by the SecretsAdapter itself in
runInDryRunTransaction() and execute() — they are not expressible in this file.
Rule reference (enforced by the adapter, documented here for transparency):
DENY  — Secret value in plaintext in command
DENY  — Exfiltration (pipe to curl/wget/nc, redirect to file)
DENY  — Wildcard delete (delete at path '/' or with '*')
DENY  — Raw output (-o yaml/-o json) on production secrets
HIGH  — kubectl get secret -o yaml (exposes base64 secrets)
HIGH  — Wildcard list on production (vault list /, gcloud secrets list)
name: secrets-default
description: Default safety policy for secret management operations (Vault, AWS SM/SSM, GCP, Azure KV, kubectl, Docker, env)

rules.description: Deny commands that pass secret values as inline CLI arguments
rules.message: Passing secret values as inline CLI arguments exposes them in shell history and process lists. Use file references or environment variable injection instead.

rules.description: Deny secret deletion without a specific target name (wildcard)
rules.message: Wildcard or untargeted secret deletion permanently destroys multiple secrets. Specify the exact secret name.

rules.description: Require approval for kubectl get secret -o yaml/-o json (exposes base64 data)
rules.message: kubectl get secret -o yaml/json outputs all secret data as base64. Use -o jsonpath to retrieve specific fields. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: Require approval for any secret deletion
rules.message: Secret deletion is irreversible. Ensure rotation is complete before deletion. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: Require approval for listing all secrets without scope limiter
rules.message: Wildcard access to secrets exposes the full secret inventory. Approval required.

rules.description: Require dry-run simulation for write/rotate operations
rules.message: Secret writes and rotations require a dry-run review before execution.

rules.description: All other secret operations require dry-run validation and explicit approval
rules.message: Secret operations require validation (dry-run) and explicit approval before execution.
